title: Treptower Park & the Soviet War Memorial - Quick Summary
items: **Treptower Park is a big riverside park in southeast Berlin, and inside it stands the Soviet War Memorial (Sowjetisches Ehrenmal), the largest Soviet monument in Germany.** It is free and always open, about a 15 minute walk from S Treptower Park (S8, S85, S9, S41 and S42).

**This is a real war cemetery, not just a monument: around 7,000 Red Army soldiers who died in the 1945 Battle of Berlin are buried here.** It opened on 8 May 1949. Visit quietly and respectfully, and it is fine to take photos.

**The memorial is built as one long straight walk that you read in sequence.** Entrance, then a grieving "Mother Homeland" statue, then two giant red granite flags with kneeling soldiers, then a sunken field lined with 16 stone sarcophagi, and finally the huge bronze soldier on his hill.

**The soldier at the end ("Der Befreier", the Liberator) is 12 metres tall and weighs about 70 tonnes.** He carries a rescued German child and rests his sword on a broken swastika. Climb the mound and turn around for the best view back down the whole axis.
**A popular Berlin story says the red granite was taken from Hitler's ruined Reich Chancellery. That is a myth.** The stone was quarried for the memorial, so do not repeat the rumour as fact.

**It doubles as a relaxed summer park on the Spree.** You can walk to the riverbank, rent a boat, or cross to the little Insel der Jugend island. Note that the nearby Spreepark amusement park is being rebuilt and reopens in spring 2027 (construction tours from April 2026), and the Archenhold Observatory is closed for now.
